QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_6

question.text: Mr. Karthik has a loss from house property of Rs.3,50,000 in FY 2025-26 (old tax regime). His other incomes are: Salary Rs.8,00,000 and Bank FD interest Rs.1,00,000. How much of the house property loss can be set off against other heads of income in the current year?

answer.text: Rs.3,50,000 — full loss can be set off against salary and interest income
Rs.2,00,000 — inter-head set-off of house property loss is capped at Rs.2,00,000 per year; balance Rs.1,50,000 is carried forward
Nil — house property loss cannot be set off against salary income
Rs.1,00,000 — only against interest income under the same head, not salary

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the old tax regime, loss from house property can be set off against other heads of income, but the inter-head set-off is limited to Rs.2,00,000 per year. The balance Rs.1,50,000 (Rs.3,50,000 - Rs.2,00,000) is carried forward for 8 assessment years to be set off only against house property income. **The Trap:** Option A ignores the Rs.2,00,000 cap introduced from AY 2018-19. Option C wrongly suggests house property loss cannot be set off against salary at all. **Cross-Exam Context:** This Rs.2,00,000 cap is frequently tested across NISM Series X-A and X-B exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For clients with multiple home loans, the Rs.2,00,000 inter-head cap is aggregate — not per property. Plan interest payments accordingly.
